description: Verso: list of names with professions in Judaeo-Arabic. The names include: Abū Ṭāhir Ibn al-ʿAnbarī; Abū l-Makārim al-[Koh]en; Sulaymān Ibn al-Zanīʿa(?), broker (simsār); his foster son (rabīb); Muhadhdhab; his servant (khadīm); Ibrāhīm Ibn al-Taʿtūʿ, banker (ṣayrafī); Abū ʿAlī, vendor (al-mutasawwiq); Abū Naṣr, seller of copper coins (yabīʿ al-fulūs); the children of Karam Ibn al-ʿUjayl; Abū Thanāʾ b. Hiba b. Nuʿmān Levi, money assayer who sells copper coins (naqqād yabīʿ al-fulūs); Mufaḍḍal and Abū l-Faḍl the children of Nabīqa(??), perfumers (ʿaṭṭārīn); Abū Saʿd b. Maḥāsin al-Shammash Levi, kaḥkī (? might be a variant of kaʿkī, however the scribe also writes kaʿkī with an ʿayin below, so this is probably a different profession); Abū Naṣr Kohen the in-law of Rabbenu Yeḥiʾel, tailor; Surūr b. Abū l-Ghayth, peddler (ṭawwāf); his paternal uncle; Abū l-Karam, peddler; his son Yaʿqūb, peddler; Abū l-Khayr, perfumer, the kinsman (? nasīb) of Umm Baqāʾ the midwife (al-qābila); Abū Isḥāq b. Avraham b. Denan(??), bookbinder (mujallild); Ṣedaqa b. Ṭāhir al-Qūṣī, pastry maker (kaʿkī); Abū l-Faraj Ibn Sumaysima, assayer (naqqād); his brother Abū l-ʿAlāʾ, silk merchant (ḥarīrī); the vendor (bayyāʿ) Mufaḍḍal the son of the fan maker (marāwiḥī) and his young son; Abū Ḥayyūn the Maghribī carpenter (najjār); his daughter's son, tailor; Hārūn al-Shāmī, interpreter (mutarjim) and porter (yaḥmil); Suwayd and Raḍiyy the Alexandrians, dyers (ṣabbāghīn); [...] the dyer the in-law of Sulaymān the dyer; Makārim b. Bū Wadīna(?) the dyer; Abū l-ʿAlāʾ the boy of al-ʿArīf Maḥāsin the dyer; Barakāt b. Bū Naṣr al-Maḥallī the dyer; Sibāʿ the spinner (al-ghazzālī) and dyer; Abū l-ʿIzz the son of the daughter of the broker (ibn bint al-dallāla), dyer; ʿIwaḍ al-Maḥallī and his foster son the dyer who lives near the Palestinian Synagogue; Yosef the Maghribī; the dyer the husband of the daughter of Umm Kufā(?); his foster son; Hilāl the dyer of red fabric (al-ṣabbāgh al-aḥmar) the foster son of Abū Riḍā al-Kohen; Sulaymān; Abū ʿUmar the Alexandrian the dyer; Abū Saʿd al-ʿAdanī the perfumer; his foster son Makārim, porter (yaḥmil); Hiba b. Tamīm the perfumer; his son Yaʿqūb; his brother's son Sulaymān b. Mūsā b. Tamīm; Abū l-Futūḥ b. Abū l-ʿIzz the dyer the liturgical poet (murahhiṭ) - may be identical with the cantor of the same name in T-S 8J17.28 (PGPID 1992); Abū l-Faraj b. Dāʾūd the brother of Yosef the dyer - may be mentioned in Bodl. MS heb. d 66/21 (PGPID 569); Wafāʾ b. Abū l-Surūr the candle maker (shammāʿ) the silk merchant (qazzāz); the boy of Khalaf b. Isḥāq the silk merchant (qazzāz), Kohen; The boy of Abū Naṣr Ibn al-Rabbāṭ; the wife of Asad; Ṭayyib b. Sulaymān the cheesemaker (jabbān), Kohen; Najīb b. Salīm the Bilbaysī, who used to be the servant (khadīm) of the son of the judge the banker; Hibatallāh Ibn al-Muṣinn, Kohen, perfumer; Ḥayyūn the Maghribī the son of the wife of Yishay al-ʿAjamī (cf. T-S AS 148.209 (PGPID 37754)); Masʿūd al-Mawṣilī; Munajjā the lame, orphan, dyer; Yeḥezqel al-Kaḥkī(?).
description_he: גב: רשימה ארוכה של שמות עם מקצועות ביהודית-ערבית - סרסורים, בנקאים, צבעים, בשמאים, חייטים, כורכי ספרים, יצרני נרות, ועוד, מתוך קהילת פוסטאט
